# compustat_global.feather

## Description
Compustat Global financial and company data downloaded from WRDS for all companies (GVKEYs) present in our transcript dataset. Contains annual financial metrics and company identifiers for international companies, unique by gvkey-fyear pairs.

## Generated by
`src/pre_query/compustat/download_compustat_global.py`

## Variables

### gvkey
Global company key - unique identifier for each company in Compustat. String/object type, preserves leading zeros.

### fyear
Fiscal year of the observation. Integer type (Int64), can contain missing values.

### costat
Company status indicator. Values: A (Active), I (Inactive). Category type.

### curcd
Currency code of the financial data (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Category type. Multiple currencies present since this is global data.

### indfmt
Industry format classification. Values: INDL (Industrial companies), FS (Financial Services). When duplicates exist for the same gvkey-fyear, INDL is prioritized over FS. Category type.

### datadate
Date of the financial data observation. Object type (date format).

### conm
Company name as recorded in Compustat. String type.

### fic
Country of incorporation code (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, DEU). Category type.

### ggroup
GICS (Global Industry Classification Standard) group code. Category type.

### gind
GICS industry code. Category type.

### gsector
GICS sector code. Category type.

### gsubind
GICS sub-industry code. Category type.

### loc
Location/domicile country code. Category type.

### sic
Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code. String type, zero-padded to 4 digits (e.g., "3241", "2800"). Used for industry classification, particularly useful for construction and cement industries where SIC provides more granular classification than GICS. Can contain missing values.

### naics
North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code. String type, zero-padded to 6 digits (e.g., "327310", "236117"). Alternative industry classification system used primarily in North America. Can contain missing values.

### emp
Number of employees in thousands. Float64 type, can contain missing values.
